title: lonchocarpus major
intro: Lonchocarpus major, commonly known as the "Madagascar almond" or "N'goma", is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Madagascar, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Lonchocarpus major include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a purgative and diuretic. Herbal preparations of the plant's seeds, leaves, and bark are often used in traditional medicine, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, although the exact methods and dosages may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
